title: Photos: Wilderness ice skating awaits out at Anchorage coastal refuge
summary: Don't let a dust of snow deter wilderness ice-skating adventures at the Anchorage Coastal Wildlife Refuge. Theres something bracing about zipping atop marsh that's been inaccessible all summer.

title: Trio of Native Kuskokwim salmon fishermen on trial found guilty
summary: Ignorance of the law is no defense, Judge Bruce Ward told the courtroom, saying that defendant Harry David had a responsibility to learn of the king salmon closure.

title: Witnesses describe moments leading to fatal shooting outside Anchorage nightclub
summary: A crowd watched the shooting that killed 27-year-old Said Beshirov early Sunday morning outside a Platinum Jaxx Halloween party, police said and witnesses said on Monday.

title: 'Skyfall' already making big bucks in theaters around the world
summary: 'Skyfall,' the 23rd James Bond film, hasn't yet hit theaters in the U.S., but it's already making a splash overseas, opening at No. 1 in 25 foreign countries around the world.

title: Looming Cook Inlet gas shortages may be crippling
summary: All the exploration going on in Cook Inlet probably will not generate gas quickly enough to avoid significant near-term shortfalls, according to the new report.

title: University of Alaska president blames student extravagance for their debt
summary: Defending the latest tuition increase, UA President Pat Gamble dismissed student debt as a choice that inexperienced youth often make in pursuit of a lifestyle that's beyond their means.

title: Why Americans should watch Larry King's debate with presidential candidates Jill Stein, Rocky Anderson, Virgil Goode and Gary Johnson
summary: Third party debate: Larry King hosted a debate with presidential candidates that comparatively few people will see, although perhaps more should.

title: The politics of the middle class
summary: Everyone claims they're in the middle, but few can define it. The middle class is the central theme of the presidential campaign.

title: Brown bear sighting reported in area of search for wounded bruin
summary: Anchorage police report a brown bear was seen early Thursday morning in the area of Rabbit Creek Road and Clarks Road, just north of Bear Valley Elementary, and just to the northeast in the area of 140th Avenue and Rabbit Creek.

title: Kodiak bear shot while raiding chicken coop
summary: Kodiak bear shot while raiding chicken coop

title: Chile will request extradition of US captain over Pinochet murders
summary: Chile wants to put Captain Ray E. Davis on trial for his role in the 1973 killings of journalist Charles Horman and student Frank Teruggi.

title: Kenai Peninsula landscape radically altered by typhoon-like storms
summary: Typhoon-like weather on the Kenai Peninsula last month caused more than $1 million in damage to roads and bridges from Seward to Homer, to say nothing of the dangers homeowners face from a landscape altered by so much rain.

title: In Alaska, whose hunting and fishing rights matter most?
summary: Food fight: Alaska Natives argue they have subsistence rights, but a race-based priority is in conflict with Alaska's constitution. Will Indian Country and Alaska -- battling for decades now -- ever work it out?

title: North of Denali, Alaska may be a warmer place this winter
summary: Sorry, Southcentral Alaskans: the state's population center is currently forecast to experience an average winter. But if you live in Fairbanks or points north of there, there may be reason to celebrate. Here's why.

title: Alaska Federation of Natives: Emphasis on politics with an eye on elections
summary: Success Beyond Barriers: The 2012 Alaska Federation of Natives convention is stoking a galvanized, active political voice for the upcoming election and beyond.

title: Police: Anchorage crime down over past three decades
summary: Crime in the most serious categories tracked by the FBI is down in Anchorage, which saw fewer crimes reported in 2011 than 1981, despite growing by more than 100,000 people, according to city officials.

title: How did air-breathing animals evolve? UAF scientists may have a clue
summary: UAF scientists may have found the ancestral trait that allowed air-breathing vertebrates to evolve from their sea-dwelling counterparts.

title: Trooper shot during traffic stop identified, suspect's autopsy released
summary: Alaska State Troopers have released the identity of a trooper injured during a Saturday morning traffic stop, and the results of an autopsy on the suspect, who stole the trooper's patrol vehicle and was found dead a mile from the scene.

title: Tale of two magmas may be to blame for explosive volcanic eruptions
summary: Scientists at the University of Southampton in the United Kingdom believe they may have figured out the trigger for some explosive volcanic eruptions by studying an oft-exploding peak in the Canary Islands.

title: French President promises to ban homework
summary: France: President Francois Hollande has vowed to ban homework, saying school work should 'be done at school, rather than at home.'

title: LGBT immigrant couples experiencing harassment sue for protection, test DOMA
summary: As the Department of Homeland Security eases up on married LGBT immigrants, five couples are suing for equal protection in a fight to overturn the Defense of Marriage Act.

title: Putin's power push makes Russia's regional elections irrelevant
summary: In Russia, regional elections were once seen as a critical test for the opposition, but they are now seen as irrelevant.

title: Man pulled from rubble of collapsed Florida garage
summary: A construction worker was pulled out alive from under a huge slab of concrete early Thursday, 13 hours after a parking garage collapsed at a Florida college, killing two others. One worker was still unaccounted for and rescuers said they expected to find another body in the rubble.

title: Juneau man agrees to torch million-dollar home
summary: David MacDonald, of Juneau, watched his million-dollar home burn to the ground, but it was for a good cause.

title: Man presumed dead after trying to retrieve canoe in Portage Lake
summary: A man is presumed dead after wading into frigid Portage Lake on Monday to retrieve a canoe that drifted away from shore, according to Alaska State Troopers.

title: Discovering -- and dining on -- the mummies of the far north
summary: The ongoing discovery of mammoth mummies in Siberia begs the question: Where are all of Alaska's mummies? The answer -- and what some scientists do with them -- might surprise you.

title: Sarah Palin spotted ... reading ... in public
summary: Enquiring minds want to know: What does former Alaska Gov. Sarah Palin really read in her free time?

title: Fancy wristwatch saves Alaskan hunter's life... or does it?
summary: The lamestream media may deserve kudos for failing to spread an obviously fictional story set in the Alaska wilderness and designed to sell high-end outdoor watches.

title: Scientist say Earth may soon lose its magnetic field
summary: Geological records show the planet's magnetic field has reversed itself before -- about 800,000 years ago -- and we're due for another. The magnetic field surrounding Earth is weakening, and scientists say it could be gone in as little as 500 years, resulting in the flip.

title: Some Ohioans wish Sarah Palin was running for president
summary: Sarah Palin's flashy style and intemperate comments, combined with an almost total lack of federal or international experience, made her something of a laughingstock in 2008. But she's still popular in Ohio.
